# Cirth v0.5.0

Cirth v0.5.0 is a guardrails release. The properties the project
advertises — what each build variant contains, the WCAG AA claim, how
the framework renders — are now verified continuously in CI instead of
by review attention, and the first runs of those checks caught real
defects in the shipped builds, all fixed here. The light theme also
adopts the warm paper canvas that previously lived only in the docs.

## Highlights

- **Build smoke tests** (`npm run check:dist`): every dist file
  re-parses with Lightning CSS and is non-empty, classless builds emit
  no class selectors, scoped builds keep every rule inside the `.cirth`
  subtree, and presets only set custom properties on theme roots.
- **Accessibility audit** (`npm run check:a11y`): axe-core with the
  WCAG 2.x A/AA rule set audits every page of the built docs site in
  both color schemes. The component demos live on those pages, so the
  framework's own AA claim is re-verified on every push. The violation
  baseline is empty — all 26 findings from the first run were fixed,
  not baselined.
- **Visual regression testing** (`npm run check:visual`): Playwright
  screenshot-diffs every docs page — full page, light and dark, at
  1440 px and 390 px — against per-platform baselines stored in Git
  LFS. Linux baselines regenerate and commit themselves via the
  `update-visual-baselines` workflow.
- **Light theme canvas is warm paper, cards are white sheets.** The
  light background moves from pure white to
  `oklch(97.4% 0.008 69.35deg)` and cards become true white, so an
  `<article>` reads as a sheet lying on the page. WCAG AA was
  re-verified against the new canvas, with two border tokens moved one
  scale step darker to keep their 3:1 non-text contrast.
- **Larger `h1` display scale** — `clamp(2.25rem, 1.7rem + 2.2vw,
  3.5rem)` — matching the reference-manual typography of the docs.
- The contribution guide now documents every quality gate and how to
  run it locally.

## Fixed

- **Scoped builds no longer leak rules outside `.cirth`.**
  `.container`/`.container-fluid`, `.grid` (and its children), the
  `:where(nav li)::before` list-semantics reboot, and the
  `[type="reset"].outline` selectors rendered unscoped and could style
  markup that never opted in.
- **Classless builds no longer ship the `.striped` table class**,
  emitted by a missing `$` in a Sass conditional.

## Breaking Changes

- If you relied on `.container`, `.grid`, or `.striped` working
  *outside* a `.cirth` scope (scoped builds) or `.striped` existing in
  the classless builds, those rules no longer apply — they were
  documented contracts the builds now actually honor. Use the default
  build if you need the class layer.
- The default light background is no longer pure white. Override
  `--cirth-background-color` if you need `#fff` back.
